message:
refactor(brain,terminal,render,docs): move snapshot_activations to Brain trait, add multi-layer terminal support, fix render type safety

This commit refactors the brain activation snapshot architecture by moving the introspection method from BrainRunner to the Brain trait, enabling cleaner separation of concerns and eliminating specialized adapter implementations. Additionally, it completes multi-layer brain visualization in the terminal with proper focus indicators and fixes several type safety issues in the render layer.

ARCHITECTURAL REFACTOR: Brain trait now owns activation snapshots

Previous architecture:
- Brain trait: core brain logic (tick, mutate, crossover)
- BrainRunner trait: thin execution wrapper with snapshot_activations hook
- Each concrete brain required specialized BrainRunner impl to provide activations
- MlpBrain had BrainRunnerAdapter<MlpBrain> impl with snapshot logic
- NeuroflowBrain had BrainRunnerAdapter<NeuroflowBrain> impl with snapshot logic
- Duplication of adapter pattern for each brain type wanting introspection

New architecture (scriptbots-brain/src/lib.rs):
- Brain trait extended with snapshot_activations(&self) -> Option<BrainActivations>
- Default implementation returns None for brains without introspection
- BrainRunnerAdapter<B: Brain> delegates to brain.snapshot_activations()
- Single generic adapter handles all brain types uniformly
- Concrete brains implement snapshot logic directly on Brain trait

Why this matters:
- Eliminates N specialized BrainRunner impls (one per brain type)
- snapshot_activations is fundamentally a Brain concern (introspecting internal state), not a Runner concern (executing ticks)
- Generic adapter now works for all brains; adding new brain types requires no adapter code
- Better alignment with trait responsibilities: Brain = internal logic, Runner = execution harness
- Reduces boilerplate from ~20 lines per brain to ~5 lines per brain with introspection

MLP refactor (scriptbots-brain/src/mlp.rs):
- Removed entire impl BrainRunner for BrainRunnerAdapter<MlpBrain> block (~27 lines)
- Added pub(crate) fn activations(&self) -> BrainActivations helper extracting 20x10 grid
- Implemented Brain::snapshot_activations calling self.activations()
- MlpBrain now self-contained: all logic in Brain impl, no external adapter glue
- activations() helper allows internal reuse (tests, debug) without going through trait boundary

NeuroFlow refactor (scriptbots-brain-neuro/src/lib.rs):
- Removed entire impl BrainRunner for BrainRunnerAdapter<NeuroflowBrain> block (~28 lines)
- Moved snapshot_activations logic into Brain impl (no longer separate trait impl)
- Changed self.brain.network to self.network (direct field access within same impl)
- Serde reflection approach unchanged: still serializes network to JSON, parses layers
- Now uses Brain trait's snapshot_activations, falling back to None on serde errors

Benefits of Brain trait ownership:
- Consistent interface: all brains expose introspection via same trait method
- Testing: can call brain.snapshot_activations() directly without boxing as runner
- Type safety: no downcasting required; Brain implementations have full type context
- Documentation: snapshot_activations appears in Brain trait docs, making capability discoverable
- Future extensions: brain implementations can add richer introspection (gradient snapshots, attention weights) via same hook

TERMINAL MULTI-LAYER SUPPORT: Complete navigation and display

Multi-layer architecture (scriptbots-app/src/terminal/mod.rs):
- Changed Snapshot::brain_activations field from Option<BrainLayerView> to brain_layers: Vec<BrainLayerView>
- Snapshot now captures all layers from BrainActivations, not just first layer
- Added BrainLayerView::vec_from_activations to convert all layers at once
- Added convert_layers helper function for clean separation

Layer indexing and navigation:
- snapshot.brain_activations_layer_indexed(idx) now returns brain_layers.get(idx) (proper indexing)
- Previously returned first layer regardless of index (ignored parameter)
- Layer cycling with `]` key now bounds-checks against brain_layers.len()
- Prevents index overflow when cycling past last layer

Focus legend display:
- Added "Focus agent #NNN  layer LL/TT  row RRR" line showing current navigation state
- NNN = focused_agent_cursor % agent_count (current agent index)
- LL = clamped activation_layer_index, TT = total layer count
- RRR = activation_row_offset (vertical scroll position in grid)
- Updates every frame, providing immediate feedback during navigation
- Positioned above brain visualization rows for spatial coherence

Why focus legend:
- Users navigating with ←→[]↑↓ keys need visual confirmation of position
- Multi-layer networks (NeuroFlow) can have 3-5 layers; knowing current layer essential
- Large activation grids (e.g., 64x64) require row offset to understand scroll position
- Agent count changes dynamically (births/deaths); showing index prevents confusion
- Debugging aid: when reporting brain visualization issues, users can specify exact coordinates

Ghosting artifact fix:
- Added frame.render_widget(Clear, frame.area()) at top of draw() method
- Clears entire frame buffer before rendering new content
- Prevents ghosting artifacts when terminal shrinks or layout changes
- Critical for dynamic content like moving sparklines and scrolling brain grids
- Clear widget ensures previous frame's pixels don't bleed into current frame

Why ghosting occurred:
- Ratatui maintains a screen buffer; widgets update regions but don't clear outside regions
- When terminal resizes smaller or panels reflow, previously-rendered content in now-unused regions persists
- Brain visualization with scrolling rows particularly prone: old rows remain visible after scrolling
- Clear widget resets buffer state, ensuring clean slate for each frame
- Performance impact minimal: Clear is lightweight (just marks buffer dirty), actual clear happens during diff

Import organization:
- Consolidated imports: widget imports grouped on single line (Block, Borders, List, ListItem, Paragraph, Sparkline, Widget, Clear)
- Added ActivationLayer import alongside BrainActivations for layer conversion
- Maintains alphabetical ordering and semantic grouping

RENDER TYPE SAFETY FIXES: Eliminate shadowing and improve API usage

paint_activation_grid refactor (scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs):
- Renamed `size` variable to `bounds_size` to avoid shadowing bounds.size field access
- Changed Bounds literal construction to Bounds::new() with point() and size() helpers
- Old: Bounds { origin: Point { x: ..., y: ... }, size: size.with_width(...).with_height(...) }
- New: Bounds::new(point(px(...), px(...)), size(px(...), px(...)))
- Eliminates intermediate Point construction and with_width/with_height chain
- Type-safe: px() wrapper ensures pixel units, preventing accidental raw number bugs

Why Bounds::new pattern:
- Matches GPUI 0.8+ idiomatic API (point(), size(), px() helpers)
- More readable: point(px(x), px(y)) vs Point { x: x, y: y }
- Compiler enforces unit types via px() wrapper, catching unit mismatches at compile time
- Consistent with other canvas painting code in render module

Canvas state cloning fix:
- Added explicit state_for_canvas clone in activation heatmap rendering
- Old: move |_, _, _| state.clone() (captured state, cloned in closure)
- New: let state_for_canvas = state.clone(); move |_, _, _| state_for_canvas.clone()
- Ensures each closure captures independent clone, preventing borrow conflicts
- Required when multiple canvases capture same layer state (grid + edges)

Child API consistency:
- Changed .children(vec![canvas_el.into(), edges_canvas.into()]) to .child(canvas_el).child(edges_canvas)
- Avoids explicit into() calls and intermediate Vec allocation
- Matches GPUI builder API conventions (chained .child() calls)
- Type inference works better with chained calls vs vec![...] coercion

paint_frame variable simplification:
- Changed separate render_left/top/right/bottom declarations to destructured tuple
- Old: let render_left = offset_x; let render_top = offset_y; ... (4 separate bindings)
- New: let (mut render_left, mut render_top, mut render_right, mut render_bottom) = (...) (single binding)
- Makes mut modifier explicit and clear all four variables are related
- Prevents accidental const/mut mismatch across related variables

DOCUMENTATION: Headless mode and environment variables

Headless terminal report example (README.md):
- Added complete CI-friendly example showing headless terminal rendering
- Environment variables:
  * SCRIPTBOTS_MODE=terminal (run in terminal renderer)
  * SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS=1 (no TTY, offscreen rendering)
  * SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_FRAMES=24 (render 24 frames then exit)
  * SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_REPORT=terminal_report.json (write JSON summary)
- CLI flags: --storage memory --threads 2 (in-memory DB, 2 threads for CI constraints)
- Output: JSON file with frames rendered, ticks simulated, births, deaths, energy stats

Why headless example matters:
- CI/CD pipelines need non-interactive simulation for regression testing
- Benchmark suites require reproducible runs with performance metrics
- Integration tests need JSON output for validation (birth/death counts, energy balance)
- Previously undocumented; users had to discover env vars by reading source code

Environment variable documentation:
- SCRIPTBOTS_RENDER_SAFE: force conservative rendering path (CPU fallback, no GPU features)
- SCRIPTBOTS_RENDER_WATERMARK: overlay diagnostics badge in canvas corner
- Both have CLI equivalents (--renderer-safe, --debug-watermark) but env vars useful for scripting
- Safe mode previously only accessible via CLI flag; env var enables wrapper scripts
- Watermark useful for recording videos/screenshots with embedded version/build info

Why environment variables:
- Wrapper scripts and launch configurations prefer env vars over reconstructing CLI args
- Containerized deployments (Docker, K8s) configure via environment, not command line
- Environment variables compose: export SCRIPTBOTS_RENDER_SAFE=1 applies to all subsequent runs
- CI systems (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) inject env vars easily; CLI args require command reconstruction

TESTING AND VALIDATION

Terminal multi-layer testing:
- Launch with NeuroFlow brain: cargo run -- --mode terminal --neuroflow-hidden 32,16
- Press `]` repeatedly: observe layer counter incrementing, brain visualization changing
- Verify layer count in focus legend matches network architecture (3 layers: input, 32, 16, output)
- Press `↑`/`↓`: observe row offset incrementing, different grid rows appearing

Brain trait refactor validation:
- Compile all brain crates: cargo build -p scriptbots-brain -p scriptbots-brain-neuro
- Run tests: cargo test -p scriptbots-brain -p scriptbots-brain-neuro
- Verify no specialized BrainRunner impls remain (grep for "impl BrainRunner for BrainRunnerAdapter")
- Check snapshot_activations callable from Brain trait: let brain: Box<dyn Brain> = ...; brain.snapshot_activations()

Render type safety verification:
- Compile render crate: cargo build -p scriptbots-render
- Check for shadowing warnings: cargo clippy -p scriptbots-render (should be clean)
- Verify Bounds::new() compiles with correct units (px() wrapper required)
- Test activation heatmap in GUI inspector: select agent, verify grid displays without panic

Headless mode validation:
- Run headless example: SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS=1 SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_FRAMES=10 SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_REPORT=test.json cargo run -p scriptbots-app -- --mode terminal --storage memory
- Check test.json exists and contains valid JSON with expected fields (frames, ticks, births, deaths, energy)
- Verify process exits cleanly after N frames (no hang)

MIGRATION NOTES

For downstream brain implementations:
- If you implemented BrainRunner for BrainRunnerAdapter<YourBrain>, remove that impl
- Add fn snapshot_activations(&self) -> Option<BrainActivations> to your Brain impl
- Return None if introspection not supported, or Some(BrainActivations { ... }) with layer data
- Generic adapter in scriptbots-brain handles the rest automatically

For code calling snapshot_activations:
- Previously: runner.snapshot_activations() (called on BrainRunner trait object)
- Now: brain.snapshot_activations() (called on Brain trait object)
- BrainRunner still has snapshot_activations for compatibility, but delegates to Brain trait
- No breaking changes for code calling via BrainRunner (adapter forwards correctly)

For terminal consumers:
- snapshot.brain_activations removed; use snapshot.brain_layers (Vec instead of Option)
- brain_activations_layer() still works (returns first layer)
- brain_activations_layer_indexed(idx) now respects index (get(idx) instead of first)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -194,6 +194,16 @@ RUST_LOG=info cargo run -p scriptbots-app
   - If emojis render as tofu/misaligned, install an emoji-capable font (e.g., Noto Color Emoji) or toggle off with `e`.
 - Narrow symbols mode: press `n` to switch to width-1 friendly symbols while keeping emoji colors off-background; helpful for strict terminals/alignment.
 
+- Headless report (CI-friendly):
+  ```bash
+  SCRIPTBOTS_MODE=terminal \
+  SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS=1 \
+  SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_FRAMES=24 \
+  SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_REPORT=terminal_report.json \
+  cargo run -p scriptbots-app -- --storage memory --threads 2
+  ```
+  This renders offscreen for N frames and writes a JSON summary (frames, ticks, births/deaths, energy stats) to `terminal_report.json`.
+
 ## Quick start (platform scripts)
 
 Use the convenience scripts in the repo root to launch ScriptBots with sensible defaults per OS. These scripts set appropriate targets, isolate build artifacts, and pick the right renderer.
@@ -387,6 +397,8 @@ cargo build -p scriptbots-brain-ml --features candle
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_REPORT` — file path to write a JSON summary from a headless run.
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_MAX_THREADS` — preferred maximum thread budget; core will cap Rayon to min of CPUs and this value (used unless `RAYON_NUM_THREADS` is already set).
  - `SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_EMOJI` — force emoji mode `1|true|yes|on` or disable with `0|false|off|no`.
+ - `SCRIPTBOTS_RENDER_SAFE` — force conservative rendering path in GUI mode (also enabled by `--renderer-safe` or `--low-power`).
+ - `SCRIPTBOTS_RENDER_WATERMARK` — overlay a tiny diagnostics watermark in the GUI canvas (also enabled by `--debug-watermark`).
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_ENABLED` — `true|false`.
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_HIDDEN` — comma-separated hidden sizes (e.g., `64,32,16`).
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_ACTIVATION` — `tanh|sigmoid|relu`.
